mnemonics.title: Waste Hierarchy Order
mnemonics.content: Please Recycle Energy Disposal (P-R-E-D): Prevention → Reuse → Recycling → Energy Recovery → Disposal

mnemonics.title: Solid Waste Sources
mnemonics.content: R.C.I.I.A.C (Residential Commercial Industrial Institutional Agricultural Construction)

mnemonics.title: Landfill Components
mnemonics.content: L.C.L.G (Liner, Cell, Leachate collection, Gas management)

acronyms.usage: Fuel produced from processed MSW used in cement kilns and power plants
Waste from residential, commercial, and institutional sources collected by municipalities
Complete process of handling solid waste from generation to disposal
Policy approach making producers responsible for end-of-life management of their products
Technologies converting waste into usable energy forms like heat, electricity, or fuel
Waste from construction, renovation, and demolition activities
Infrastructure for separating waste at source for better recycling

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted triangle with widest part at top labeled 'Prevention' narrowing down to 'Disposal' at bottom - shows priority order from most to least preferred
Draw layers from bottom up: compacted clay liner → geotextile → gravel drainage → waste layer → daily cover soil → gas collection pipes → final cover
Show circular flow: Organic Waste → Shredding → Pile Formation → Microbial Action → Turning/Aeration → Curing → Compost Output
Three concentric circles - inner 'Reduce' (smallest but most important), middle 'Reuse', outer 'Recycle' - showing priority order

analogies.analogy: Just like a cake has layers (base, filling, frosting), a landfill has layers of waste separated by daily soil cover, with a protective base (liner) like a cake pan
Like a high-temperature oven that 'cooks' waste at 850-1200°C, reducing volume dramatically and generating heat energy like a power plant
Like a compost bin is like a stomach for nature - microorganisms 'digest' organic waste and convert it into nutrient-rich 'compost fertilizer'
Just as hot water passing through tea bag extracts color and flavor, water percolating through waste extracts pollutants forming leachate
